  ARM is going to be the tech to watch in this space IMHO, especially with NVIDIA's upcoming weight behind it.  Reply
      Is it possible to add another "benchmark" - ESX server workload? Like, running 8-16-32-64 VMs all with some workload...  Reply
  As we're rebuilding our server test suite, I'll be looking into more diverse benchmarks to include. It's a long process that needs a lot of thought and possibly resources so it's not always evident to achieve.  Reply
  When do we think this will be available to order? Also wondering the same about Ice Lake SP availability but seems it's hard to know for sure.   Reply
  Looks decent, though the price and TDP increases make it look less appealing at the high end than it otherwise would. Perks of reusing the same process for two generations, I suppose.  Going to be a very interesting compare against Altra Max.  Reply
title: 审查3RD-Gen Epyc米兰筹码显示AMD继续加强和维持其主要竞争对手，英特尔的一个非常卑鄙的片面性能优势
title_en: Review of the 3rd-gen EPYC Milan chips shows AMD continues to strengthen and maintain a very stark one-sided performance advantage against its main rival, Intel

note:
AMD表示，它正在为Intel提出绩效和价格的酒吧，同时在数据中心推出了第三代EPYC处理器的新安全功能，该公司尚未为云，企业和高性能计算工作负载提供最广泛的阵容。
 新的处理器代码为米兰，周一宣布，仅在英特尔将其启动其下一代Xeon可扩展处理器，指定冰湖，在数据中心市场上建立新的刷新战斗时期这仍然由英特尔主导。
 基于Santa Clara，Calif.的芯片制造商表示，来自戴尔技术的100多个新的服务器平台，惠普·帕特德企业，Supermicro和其他供应商将支持新的处理器。亚马逊Web服务和Microsoft Azure等云服务提供商还计划加快使用AMD EPYC处理器的使用，这些处理器预计将在今年年底之前为400多个云实例提供电量。
  AMD表示，19个新处理器，功能高达64个核心和4.1 GHz的频率，扩展了芯片制造者的竞争优势，比2019年的第二代EPYC罗马阵容进一步拓展，并提供了英特尔第二代Xeon可扩展的“头部和肩部”的性能处理器，包括与去年的竞争对手最新的级联湖刷新零件。 （该公司表示，它没有来自英特尔即将推出的冰湖处理器的公共表现数字。）
 但是，根据公司高管的说法，新米兰处理器也在继续EPYC的“完美”总体拥有成本（TCO）故事，使数据中心运营商更加核心核心计数的其他经济效益，以美元的价格更具表现。
 “第三族EPYC是行业中最高性能的服务器处理器，在套接字级别和每核水平时，当您查看每个核心计数边界时，AMD的EPYC产品管理公司副总裁Ram Peddibhotla说，在预先说明中。 “由于这种表现，第三届Gen Epyc是HPC和企业的最佳选择，在那里它在提供最先进的安全性和数据保护的同时推动大量TCO值。”
 根据Peddibhotla的说法，米兰的另一个强度是它可以在具有BIOS更新的第二代EPYC处理器的现有服务器平台上运行，而不会丢失任何米兰的新功能，这意味着合作伙伴更容易升级。他补充说，与英特尔的方法相比，这将需要合作伙伴采用新的服务器平台，以即将到来的冰湖芯片，另一个，鹰溪流，与蓝宝石急流芯片一起出现。 
“第三局EPYC处理器具有完整的平台杠杆，这减少了合作伙伴在采用第三阶段ePYC方面的投资，而且它也减少了他们的最终客户[资格]周期，”Peddibhotla说。
 丹麦纳拉纳领导AMD的EPYC业务，表示，米兰处理器的速度比英特尔的第二代Xeon可扩展芯片为企业，云和HPC工作负载，基于AMD的内部测试使用SpecJBB2015，SpectRate2017_Int_Base和SpectRate2017_FP_Base进行了两倍。基准工具分别。
 根据McNamara的说法，为了鼓励采用，将由云，硬件和软件供应商的大型云，硬件和软件供应商以及用于解决各种业务需求的电器和参考架构来支持。在支持米兰的80多个供应商中，是亚马逊Web服务，思科，Cloudera，Docker，戴尔技术，谷歌云，惠威特Packard Enterprise，IBM云，瞻博网络，联想，微软Azure，Nutanix，Oracle，Red Hat，SUSE和VMware 。
 “我们真的试图解决问题，”麦克纳拉斯说。 “这不仅仅是关于提供性能和TCO，但它也借给它的解决方案，让客户可以非常迅速加速我们所带来的价值，这真的是米兰向前迈进的重点。”
 米兰基于AMD的7纳米ZEN 3架构，最后举行了AMD的Ryzen 5000台式机加工器的推出。 ZEN 3对先前ZEN 2架构的改进包括每个时钟的指令增加19％，以及处理器的每个小芯片中的较大L3缓存，其提供多达八个核心访问相同的32 MB缓存。
 新的处理器还扩展了内存通道的灵活性，超出了第三代六个通道的第三个选项的四个和八通道选项。
 至于安全性，米兰改善了EPYC的安全加密虚拟化功能，其中第二代具有名为Secure Nested Paging的新功能，为客户操作系统提供了对不受信任的虚拟机管理程序的新保护。此外，新的处理器具有阴影堆栈，一个新的硅级保护，针对一种名为Control-Flow劫持攻击的恶意软件，允许攻击者操纵内存以修改现有代码。 
其他功能包括128个PCIe 4.0连接，内存容量4 TB，并支持最大DDR4频率为3,200 MHz。米兰的所有功能都存在于整个堆栈的处理器中，高管表示是他们与英特尔区别的另一种方式。
 AMD正在将其19个米兰处理器分成三组，以解决数据中心市场上的不同需求：核心性能，为客户需要更高频率和每个核心缓存的客户，需要高核心和线程计数的客户的核心密度，平衡和优化适用于需要组合性能和总体优化成本的客户。四个处理器专为单套接服务器设计，而剩余的适用于双套接字服务器。
 Peddibhotla表示，阵容代表了AMD的“以客户为中心的方式”到数据中心市场，允许客户找到其工作负载的正确处理器，而无需为他们不需要的功能或功能支付更多。他补充说，AMD将继续向新零件销售第二代EPYC处理器。
 根据CRN分析，米兰处理器的卷定价表示来自第二代EPYC阵容的可比模型的增加5-19％。 AMD发言人表示，该公司将第三代EPYC处理器定位为提供“每个核心边界的最终性能”，而第二代将提供“奇妙的性能和价值”。
 核心性能组是去年推出的高频EPYC 7FX2处理器AMD的刷新，例如企业工作负载，例如超电流基础设施，关系数据库和商业HPC。四个处理器包括八个，16-，24和32个核心选项，升高频率为4 GHz，八个核心模型的额外100 MHz。
 核心密度组专为云，HPC和高端企业段设计。它由五个高核心计数处理器组成，具有48-，56和64核心选项。一个64核模型用于单套接字服务器，56核处理器代表前一代的新核心计数选项。
 Peddibhotla的说法，均衡和优化的集团旨在为“企业核心”，包括超电流基础设施，数据分析，数据库，协作，Web和Java工作负载。阵容由10个处理器组成，其中有16个，24，28和32核心计数的选项，其中三个是单套接字服务器，其静止用于双插槽。 
Peddibhotla表示，米兰处理器根据使用SpectRate2017_Int_Base云基准测试的估计，根据相对性能的估计，对Intel的第二代Xeon可扩展阵容进行强烈竞争。例如，AMD的新款28核EPYC 7453处理器新的核心计数选项提供略高于英特尔旗舰28核心Xeon Gold 6258R，但EPYC 7453的批量定价为每芯片1,570美元，不到Xeon的一半。他说，黄金6258欧元推荐的客户价格，使其成为“占主导地位的性能”比例。
 进一步下降堆栈，Peddibhotla说，AMD的$ 650 16核心EPYC 7282来自第二代已经优于英特尔900美元的1600美元Xeon Silver 4216.但是在第三代中引入了三个新的16核选项，AMD的性能优势进入了更进一步，他补充说，尽管它们比英特尔部分贵183美元​​至2,621美元。
 超越米兰的价格性能优势，Peddibhotla证明了第三代EPYC的核心密度和性能如何降低数据中心的总体拥有成本。他表明，运行64核AMD的32台服务器7763S可以提供​​与运行28核Intel Xeon Gold 6258RS的63服务器相同的整数性能水平，将服务器数量缩小为近一半，同时将空间减少25％和35倍百分。根据Peddibhotla的数据，这增加了35％的总体拥有成本减少了35％。
 “在许多情况下，还有软件许可，也可以分层，”他说。 “高性能意味着IT决策者可以购买更少的软件许可证，这是一个增加的奖金，即在我们在此显示的这种裸金属TCO储蓄上成为乘数倍增效果。”
note_en:
AMD said it’s raising the bar for performance and price against Intel while introducing new security features in the data center with the launch of its third-generation EPYC processors, which represent the company’s broadest lineup yet for cloud, enterprise and high-performance computing workloads.
 The new processors, code-named Milan, were announced Monday, only a few weeks before Intel is set to launch its next-generation Xeon Scalable processors, code-named  Ice Lake, setting up a new refresh battle over customers in a data center market that is still  dominated by Intel.
 The Santa Clara, Calif.-based chipmaker said more than 100 new server platforms from Dell Technologies, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Supermicro and other vendors will support the new processors. Cloud service providers like Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure also plan to ramp up their use of AMD EPYC processors, which are expected to power more than 400 cloud instances by the end of the year.
  AMD said the 19 new processors, which feature up to 64 cores and 4.1 GHz in frequency, extend the chipmaker’s competitive advantage further than 2019’s  second-generation EPYC Rome lineup and provides performance that is “head and shoulders” above Intel’s second-generation Xeon Scalable processors, which includes its rival’s latest  Cascade Lake refresh parts from last year. (The company said it did not have public performance figures from Intel’s upcoming Ice Lake processors.)
 But the new Milan processors are also continuing EPYC’s “perfect” total cost of ownership (TCO) story, according to company executives, giving data center operators more performance for their dollar on top of other economic benefits that come with higher core counts.
 “Third-gen EPYC is the highest-performance server processor in the industry, at the socket level and at the per-core level when you look at every core-count boundary,” said Ram Peddibhotla, AMD’s corporate vice president of EPYC product management, in a pre-briefing. “And as a result of that performance, third-gen EPYC is the best choice for cloud, for HPC and enterprise, where it drives significant TCO value while providing state-of-the-art security and data protection.”
 Another strength of Milan, according to Peddibhotla, is that it can run on existing server platforms for second-generation EPYC processors with a BIOS update, without losing any of Milan’s new capabilities, which means that it will be easier for partners to upgrade. This, he added, contrasts with Intel’s approach, which will require partners to adopt a new server platform, Whitley, with the upcoming Ice Lake chips, and another, Eagle Stream, with the  Sapphire Rapids chips that come after.
 “The third-gen EPYC processors come with complete platform leverage, and this reduces our partners’ investment in terms of adopting third-gen EPYC, and it reduces their end customer [qualification] cycles as well,” Peddibhotla said.
 Dan McNamara, a former Intel executive who leads AMD’s EPYC business, said the Milan processors are two times faster than Intel’s second-generation Xeon Scalable chips for enterprise, cloud and HPC workloads based on internal tests conducted by AMD using the SPECJjbb2015, SPECrate2017_int_base and SPECrate2017_fp_base benchmark tools, respectively.
 To encourage adoption, the processors will be supported by a large and growing ecosystem of cloud, hardware and software vendors as well as appliances and reference architectures that address various business needs, according to McNamara. Among the 80-plus vendors supporting Milan are Amazon Web Services, Cisco, Cloudera, Docker, Dell Technologies, Google Cloud, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, IBM Cloud, Juniper Networks, Lenovo, Microsoft Azure, Nutanix, Oracle, Red Hat, SUSE and VMware.
 “We’re really trying to solve IT problems,” McNamara said. “It’s not just about delivering performance and TCO, but it’s also honing it with a solution set on top of it, so that customers can very quickly accelerate the value that we’re bringing, and that’s really the focus of Milan going forward.”
 Milan is based on AMD’s 7-nanometer Zen 3 architecture that debuted last fall with the launch of AMD’s  Ryzen 5000 desktop processors. Zen 3’s improvements over the previous Zen 2 architecture includes a 19 percent increase in instructions per clock as well as a larger L3 cache in each chiplet of the processor that gives as many as eight cores access to the same 32 MB cache.
 The new processors also expand flexibility for memory channels, going beyond the four- and eight-channel options from the second generation with a third option for six channels.
 As for security, Milan improves upon EPYC’s Secure Encrypted Virtualization capabilities from the second generation with a new feature called Secure Nested Paging that provides new protection for a guest operating system against an untrusted hypervisor. In addition, the new processors come with Shadow Stack, a new silicon-level protection against a form of malware called control-flow hijacking attacks that allows attackers to manipulate memory to modify existing code.
 Other features include 128 lanes of PCIe 4.0 connectivity, 4 TB in memory capacity and support for a maximum DDR4 frequency of 3,200 MHz. All features in Milan are present in the entire stack of processors, which executives said is another way they differentiate from Intel’s.
 AMD is splitting its 19 Milan processors into three groups to address different needs across the data center market: core performance for customers that need higher frequency and more cache per core, core density for customers that need high core and thread counts, and balanced and optimized for customers that need a mix of performance and total cost of ownership optimization. Four of the processors are designed for single-socket servers while the remaining are for dual-socket servers.
 Peddibhotla said the lineup represents AMD’s “customer-centric approach” to the data center market, allowing customers to find the right processor for their workloads without needing to pay more for features or capabilities that they don’t need. He added that AMD will continue to sell its second-generation EPYC processors alongside the new parts.
 The volume pricing for the Milan processors represents an increase of 5-19 percent over comparable models from the second-generation EPYC lineup, according to a CRN analysis. An AMD spokesperson said the company is positioning the third-generation EPYC processors as providing the “ultimate performance at each core boundary” while the second-generation will provide “fantastic performance and value.”
 The core performance group is a refresh of the high-frequency  EPYC 7Fx2 processors AMD introduced last year for enterprise workloads, such as hyperconverged infrastructure, relational databases and commercial HPC. The four processors consist of eight-, 16-, 24- and 32-core options with a boost frequency of 4 GHz and an additional 100 MHz for the eight-core model.
 The core density group is designed for cloud, HPC and high-end enterprise segments. It consists of five high-core count processors with 48-, 56- and 64-core options. One 64-core model is for single-socket servers, and the 56-core processor represents a new core-count option from the previous generation.
 The balanced and optimized group is designed for the “heart of the enterprise,” including hyperconverged infrastructure, data analytics, database, collaboration, web and Java workloads, according to Peddibhotla. The lineup consists of 10 processors with options for 16-, 24-, 28- and 32-core counts, and three of them are for single-socket servers with the rest meant for dual-socket.
 Peddibhotla said the Milan processors provide strong competition against Intel’s second-generation Xeon Scalable lineup across the stack, based on estimates for relative performance using the SPECrate2017_int_base cloud benchmark. For instance, AMD’s new 28-core EPYC 7453 processor, a new core-count option, provides slightly higher performance than Intel’s flagship 28-core Xeon Gold 6258R, but the EPYC 7453’s volume pricing is $1,570 per chip, less than half of the Xeon Gold 6258R’s $3,950 recommended customer pricing, giving it a “dominant performance-per-dollar” ratio, he said.
 Further down the stack, Peddibhotla said, AMD’s $650 16-core EPYC 7282 from the second generation already outperforms Intel’s $900 16-core Xeon Silver 4216. But with the introduction of three new 16-core options in the third generation, AMD’s performance advantage goes even further, he added, though they are $183 to $2,621 more expensive than Intel’s part.
 Beyond Milan’s price-performance advantages, Peddibhotla demonstrated how the higher core density and performance of third-generation EPYC can reduce the total cost of ownership for data centers. He showed that 32 servers running 64-core AMD EPYC 7763s can provide the same level of integer performance as 63 servers running 28-core Intel Xeon Gold 6258Rs, cutting the number of servers by nearly half while reducing space by 25 percent and power by 35 percent. That amounts to an estimated 35 percent reduction in total cost of ownership over four years, according to Peddibhotla.
 “In many cases, there is software licensing that’s layered in as well,” he said. “High performance would mean that IT decision makers can purchase fewer software licenses, and that’s an added bonus that becomes a multiplier effect on top of this bare-metal TCO savings that we’re showing here.”

在遇到新的时，我们都试图找到共同点。我巧妙地将几个网球上网上的网球＆amp;看看他们回来了什么，或者有时只是拼命地扔在墙上的意大利面。看什么棍子。如果谈话转向工作，我已经开发出了一个问题来过滤潜在的朋友。他们告诉我他们的工作是什么，我会简而言之问：
  他们的回答将它们非常准确地放在我的人身上＆lt;  - ＆gt;漫画谱，分布几乎是二进制的。喜欢，有些人刚刚决定他们是一个“x”，而作为一个“x”，他们会给我“x”的大多数问题的答案。这些人根本没有乐趣，特别是在工作。
 我的问题在办公室里也不起作用，因为即使是“真正的”人们也可能撒谎在他们的工作时喜欢他们的工作，所以我们需要一个不同的过滤问题。事实证明，对于任何问题，就足够了，只是看看他们是否具体回答。 “我们有y吗？”
 如果答案以“是”或“否”以外的任何东西开始，那么该人员开始看起来像一个漫画和amp; MLDR，或者至少他们选择自己作为漫画作为漫画。
  当有人用“Y非常重要”的答案开始时，他们可能会认为自己是CEO Trabpein一个助理程序 - 经理的身体。公开讨论细节会对他们的个人品牌产生负面影响，因此在面对尖锐的问题时，他们会出现摘要。如果您要求他们使用他们的智慧来帮助您优先考虑，那么您将获得更多的讲座，而不是答案，因为他们只是通过您可以通过这么多的更多镜片的影响来了解所有内容的影响。
 一个有用的诀窍是要求他们反优先考虑 - 这是你刚才提到的这项4个举措，这是最不重要的？这适用于每个人，但对于从这些人的讲话中特别有用。
  如果他们的答案开始“我们的跨职能理事会决定＆amp; mldr”，那么我通常会问他们认为仲裁庭的决定。如果他们不能给我个人意见，我假设我不和一个人说话，而是一些“信使”的头像，他们选择发送到办公室代替他们的实际自我。 
这些导管类型基本上存在于每个公司 - 我在少于10人的地方工作，一些人类的人认为这是一个有效的风险避免策略和amp; mldr，它很伤心。我认为有一些涉及的货物崇拜，大球队的好经理实际上无法与每个人一起辩论一切。
 因此，良好的经理避免以过程和amp的名义辩论辩论;纪律。那么，坏员工错误地认为，管理者如何回应难题，因此他们慷慨地过度应用了与他们的同龄人的每一项对话都过度应用了模式。就像他们正在做的“连衣裙 - 工作 - 你想要的“事情，但是用他们的词汇。
 在使用导管时清楚地定义小型任务是很重要的，因此它们更加难以躲避责任。但是，他们实际上非常擅长沟通他们不想要责任的事情 - 所以，如果你必须制造争议的决定，那么有一个导管宣布它使它看起来比你自己的法令大声更为“具体”。
 ^^老实说，我在这里到达。导管只是吮吸，因为他们避免了“风险”的严重思考。
  如果答案从“在我的旧公司，我们做了不同的话，那么＆amp; mldr”，那么恭喜你，你有一位办公室 - 手头的办公室。这些人真的直截了当 - 他们可以帮助你避免一些明显的错误，但往往会发言很高的关于一切的信念，所以辨别来自噪音的信号很难。
 他们通常可以获得上层管理的信心，因为他们从经验中谈论，而不是猜测。然而，ITCan与他们一起工作很难，特别是当他们拒绝考虑一个新颖的想法时，而是拍摄它下降的射击从过去经历的无底颤抖的箭头。
 当有人形成他们身份的基础时，他们的身份比其他人更多的经验，他们会感到比大多数人更加严厉的遗传争执。如果您发现自己与他们一起辩论的辩论，请向他们提供一个备受态度和amp; MLDR不要去杀戮射击，而是在必要时脱升升级情况，甚至更脆弱的泥泞。 
“它实际上只是因为我们的系统中的一个奇怪的怪癖，x在当然，通常是正确的。您是否认为重构我们的系统以消除这种怪异的值得？“比“不为本”好多了。在我们公司，它实际上是这样做的。哈。我接到你了。你错了，我是对的，Booga Booga。“
  当然，有些人实际上只会在简单＆amp答案问题。诚实，如果你问，并提供丰富的背景。我最喜欢的人，我实际上认为工程师识别它们的良好直觉。在这种感觉中，我们可以比我们获得信誉更具情感识字:)
 在Dropbox中，我实际上看到了2个极端例子。我们的自助餐厅工人之一，从字面上问我我的生命统治我们的第一次谈话，而且我们比“哦，欧洲友谊，2天到星期五2天！”另一个例子实际上是我们的首席执行官 - 我认为公共出场有一定的漫画，＆amp是必需的;赚取的人，但他有时似乎在公司全押会议上真正讲话（委员会书面编写的陈述）。我相信有一个真正的人，暗示该公司最大的问题＆amp;诚实地说话，有时候是一个错误。但是，作为工程师，我真的很欣赏透明度。
  我认为大多数人与漫画一起划分的人这样做，因为他们认为“世界”威尔多夫洛奖励这种行为 - 也许他们是对的。然而，一旦他们佩戴太长时间的面具，我认为它真的很有身份的一部分。一旦身份层硬化，越来越困难向他们大脑的欺诈思想部分送到头部。
 所以，如果你正在考虑成为一个漫画，就不要这样做。很有趣。快点。
note_en:
When meeting someone new, we all try to find common-ground. I’ll cleverly lob a few tennis balls over the net & see what theyhit back, or sometimes just desperately throw spaghetti at the wall & see what sticks. If the conversation turns to work,I’ve developed a go-to question to filter for potential friends. After they tell me what their job is, I’ll simply ask:
  Their answer places them pretty accurately on my person <-> caricature spectrum, and the distribution is almost binary. Like, some peoplehave just decided that they are an “X”, and as an “X”, they’ll give me the “X”-appropriate answer to most questions.These people are no fun at all, especially at work.
 My question doesn’t work as well in the office, because even “real” people might lie about liking their job  while at their job,so we need a different filter-question. As it turns out, just about any question will suffice&mldr Just see if they answer it specifically. “Do we have Y?”
 If the answer begins with anything other than “yes” or “no”, then the person starts to look like a caricature&mldr or, at least,they’re choosing to present themselves to you as a caricature.
  When someone pads the beginning of their answer with “Y is very important&mldr”, they probably see themselves as a CEO trappedin an associate-program-manager’s body. Publicly discussing details would negatively impact their personal brand, and so theyalways go abstract when faced with a pointed question. If you ask them to use their wisdom to help you prioritize, you’lltend to get more lectures than answers, because they just understand the impact of everything through so many more lensesthan you ever could.
 One useful trick is to ask them to anti-prioritize – of these 4 initiatives you just mentioned, which is the  least important?This works for everyone, but is especially useful for condensing lectures from these folks.
  If their answer begins “Our cross-functional council of elders decided that&mldr”, then I’ll usually ask what  they thinkof the tribunal’s decision. If they can’t give me a personal opinion, I assume that I’m not talking to a person, but rather some sort-of“messenger” avatar they’re choosing to send to the office in place of their actual self.
 These conduit-types exist in basically every company – I’ve worked at places with less than 10 people, and some humansalways decide that this is a valid risk-avoidance strategy&mldr and it’s sad. I think there’s some cargo-culting involved,where good managers of large teams actually can’t debate everything with everyone.
 So, the good manager avoids debates in the name of process & discipline. Then, the bad employee mistakenly believes thisis just how managers respond to hard questions, so they generously over-apply the pattern, out-of-context, to every conversation with their peers.It’s like they’re doing the “dress-for-the-job-you-want” thing, but with their vocabulary.
 It’s important to clearly define small tasks when working with conduits, so they have a harder time dodging responsibility.However, they’re actually really good at communicating things they don’t want responsibility for – so, if you have to makea controversial decision, having a conduit announce it makes it seem more “concrete” than if you yell the decree yourself.
 ^^honestly, I’m reaching here. Conduits just suck because they avoid “risky” critical-thinking at all costs.
  If the answer begins with “At my old company, we did Y differently&mldr”, then congrats, you’ve got a veteran-of-office-lifeon your hands. These people are really straight-forward – they can help you avoid some obvious mistakes, but often speakwith a high level of conviction about  everything, so it’s tough to discern the signal from the noise.
 They can often gain the confidence of upper-management because they talk from experience, not speculation. However, itcan be tough to work alongside them, especially when they refuse to even consider a novel idea, but rather shoot it downby drawing a never-before-seen arrow from their bottomless quiver of past experiences.
 When someone forms the basis of their identity on having more experience than everyone else, they will feel the stingof a lost argument more harshly than most. If you find yourself publicly “winning” a debate with them, it is criticallyimportant to give them an out&mldr don’t go for the kill-shot, but instead de-escalate the situation, and even muddy thewaters if necessary.
 “It’s actually only because of a weird quirk in our system that X exists at all – of course, normally,you’d be exactly right. Do you think it’s worth it to refactor our system to eliminate this weirdness?” Is so much better than“Nope. At our company, it actually DOES work this way. Ha. I got you. You’re wrong, I’m right, booga booga.”
  Of course, some people will actually just answer questions concisely & honestly, and provide rich context if you ask. Theseare my favorite people, and I actually think that engineers have a good intuition for identifying them.In that sense, we can be more emotionally-literate than we get credit for :)
 At Dropbox, I’ve actually seen 2 extreme examples of this. One of our cafeteria-workers literally asked me about my life-planduring our first conversation, and we had a much more interesting talk (and friendship) than “ooh man, 2 more days to Friday!”The other example is actually our CEO – I think that there is a certain caricature required for public appearances & earningscalls, but he sometimes seems to speak genuinely at company all-hands meetings (between committee-written prepared statements).Like, I believe there’s an actual person who is critically considering the company’s biggest problems & speaking very honestly aboutthem, sometimes to a fault. But, as an engineer, I  really appreciate the transparency.
  I think that most people who layer themselves with caricatures do so because they believe that “the world” willsomehow reward such behavior – and maybe they’re right. However, once they wear the mask for too long, I think that it reallybecomes a part of their identity. Once that identity layer hardens, it’s increasingly difficult to send an idea into thecritical-thinking portion of their brain.
 So, if you’re thinking of becoming a caricature, just don’t do it. Be fun. Come on.
title: 漫画（2020）

note:
柏林（路透社） - 德国将停止管理AstraZeneca的Covid-19疫苗，这是卫生部的发言人在星期一表示，使德国最新的欧洲国家暂停了受收入的报告。
 该部门表示，该决定遵循德国保罗Ehrlich Institute的建议，德国负责疫苗的权限。
  “在保罗EHRLICH研究所提出的建议之后，政府谨慎，停止了Astrazeneca疫苗的管理，”该部在一份声明中表示，加入卫生部长Jens Spahn将在4次新闻发布会上提供进一步的详细信息PM当地时间（GMT）。
 在从丹麦和挪威可能的严重副作用的报告后，几个欧盟国家呼吁将Astrazeneca疫苗停止，包括出血和血栓。
  上周，德国罗伯特科赫传染病研究所的洛萨威尔人表示，没有证据表明，接受疫苗的患者比类似年龄组的患者更有可能遭受血液条件。
 到目前为止，英国的许多人都收到了英国瑞典制药公司的疫苗。
note_en:
BERLIN (Reuters) - Germany will stop administering AstraZeneca’s COVID-19 vaccine, a spokesman for the Health Ministry said on Monday, making Germany the latest of several European countries to pause following reports of recipients being taken ill.
 The ministry said the decision followed a recommendation from the Paul Ehrlich Institute, Germany’s authority in charge of vaccines.
  “Following a recommendation from the Paul Ehrlich Institute, the government is, out of caution, halting the administration of the AstraZeneca vaccine,” the ministry said in a statement, adding that Health Minister Jens Spahn would provide further details at a news conference at 4 pm local time (1500 GMT).
 Several EU countries have called a halt to the AstraZeneca vaccine after reports from Denmark and Norway of possible serious side-effects, including bleeding and blood clots.
  Last week, Lothar Wieler, head of Germany’s Robert Koch Institute for Infectious Diseases, said there was no evidence that patients who received the vaccine were more likely than patients of a similar age group to suffer blood conditions.
 Many millions of people in Britain have so far received the British-Swedish pharma company’s vaccine.
title: 德国停留Astrazeneca接种疫苗
